narrative:
Lewis Ferguson is a central midfield player for the United Kingdom. His overall rating of 7.26/10 places him in the top 1% of central midfields at this tournament. This rating is composed of 54% from club form and 46% from this World Cup, reflecting his consistent performance.

Ferguson demonstrates significant defensive prowess, averaging 4.0 tackles and 1.333 interceptions per 90 minutes at this World Cup. In contrast, his attacking contributions are minimal, with expected goals per 90 minutes at 0.017 and expected assists per 90 minutes at 0.044. These creative numbers are lower than his club level output, where his xG per 90 min was 0.057 and xA per 90 min was 0.069.

Ferguson clearly operates as a defensive anchor in his national team's midfield. Scouts and fans should continue to observe his ball-winning ability and positional discipline in remaining matches. His impact is primarily in disrupting opposition play rather than driving offensive actions.
